architecture.summary: KBLaM augments a pre-trained LLM (Llama3 8B) with structured knowledge bases by encoding KB triples into fixed-length key-value vector pairs (knowledge tokens) via a pre-trained sentence encoder (ada-002) followed by learned linear adapters. These knowledge tokens are injected into each attention layer of the LLM through a rectangular attention mechanism that allows prompt tokens to attend to all knowledge tokens while knowledge tokens do not attend to each other or themselves. The system operates end-to-end without external retrieval modules, enabling dynamic KB updates and linear-scaling memory overhead with KB size.
trained_components: linear key adapters (one per attention layer)
linear value adapters (one per attention layer)
learnable query heads (one per attention layer)

biblio.title: KBLaM: Knowledge Base augmented Language Model

claims.statement: KBLaM enables integrating a large KB of more than 10K triples into an 8B pre-trained LLM of only 8K context window on one single A100 80GB GPU
KBLaM's computational overhead scales linearly with KB size rather than quadratically
KBLaM shows performance comparable to in-context learning on synthetic data while using much smaller memory footprint
On in-distribution synthetic data, KBLaM's attention achieves high top-1 and top-5 accuracy in identifying relevant triples
On out-of-distribution Enron data, KBLaM shows degraded performance compared to synthetic data but still performs better than zero-shot baseline
KBLaM degrades slower in precision for detecting unanswerable questions as KB size increases compared to in-context learning

claims.dataset: synthetic KB validation set and Enron KB
claims.statement: KBLaM's attention matrix is interpretable and exhibits retrieval behavior without explicit regularization, with top-K triples by attention score forming supporting evidence

claims.dataset: synthetic KB training (120K triples), validated on Enron
claims.statement: KBLaM learns linear adapters through instruction tuning on fully synthetic data while generalizing to real out-of-distribution data
